mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and rising household income expectations, which drive equity valuations higher through increased consumer spending and corporate earnings growth over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 138265.0 as of April 2013 reflects post-recession recovery momentum
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with accelerating labor market rebound
failure_condition: Payroll growth exceeds 150k persons in > 70% of months yet 20-day forward returns remain negative or flat (mean return < 0.5%) in those periods, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) measured month-over-month indicates strengthening consumer demand and forward earnings visibility for discretionary-exposed equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings expectations revise upward.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 380230.0 million as of April 2013 shows sustained consumer activity
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with consumer confidence stabilization
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month growth positive in > 65% of periods yet 60-day forward returns are negative or below 1% annualized in those periods, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 4.5% signals monetary tightening that raises discount rates and borrowing costs for corporations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as market reprices risk premiums.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.94% as of April 2013 indicates elevated policy rates
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive policy stance
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% in > 60% of months yet 5-day forward returns remain positive (mean > 0.2%) in those periods, or Rank IC > -0.03 (i.e., signal direction reverses).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth combined with stable or declining unemployment signals supply-side capacity expansion and labor market tightness that supports corporate profit margins and equity valuations over a 20-day horizon through improved operational efficiency and pricing power.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 114.4254 and UNRATE at 4.7% as of April 2013 reflect balanced supply-labor dynamics
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with synchronized supply and labor market improvement
failure_condition: INDPRO growth positive and UNRATE stable or declining in > 70% of periods yet 20-day forward returns are negative or below 0.3% in those joint conditions, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1.2 million annualized units signal strong residential investment demand and forward construction employment, which cascades into multiplier effects on consumer spending and equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1331.0 thousand units as of April 2013 exceeds threshold
regime_hint: housing recovery phase with accelerating residential construction
failure_condition: HOUST exceeds 1200k units in > 65% of months yet 60-day forward returns are negative or below 0.8% annualized in those triggered periods, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI (CPIAUCSL) rising above 2.5% year-over-year combined with elevated federal funds rates signals monetary policy constraint that reduces real asset valuations and produces negative equity returns over a 20-day horizon as investors demand higher risk premiums.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 207.738 and FEDFUNDS at 4.94% as of April 2013
regime_hint: stagflationary pressure with simultaneous inflation and tight monetary policy
failure_condition: Both CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS > 4.0% in > 60% of periods yet 20-day forward returns are positive or above 0.5% in those joint conditions, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions and forward earnings risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reduce equity exposure.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7% as of April 2013 provides baseline for deterioration detection
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early-cycle slowdown
failure_condition: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3-month windows in > 50% of rolling periods yet 20-day forward returns remain positive (mean > 0.3%) in those triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) signals broad-based demand expansion that supports equity valuations through higher earnings growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon when both demand indicators strengthen simultaneously.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11520.1 billion and RSAFS at 380230.0 million as of April 2013 reflect synchronized demand growth
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across consumption and output
failure_condition: GDPC1 and RSAFS both show positive growth in > 70% of periods yet 60-day forward returns are negative or below 0.5% annualized in those joint conditions, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
